items.title: U.S. grocery slowdown deepens as shoppers buy fewer items, raising pressure on food companies
items.summary: New data show the U.S. grocery slowdown is deepening as shoppers buy fewer items, forcing grocers and brands to compete harder on price and value.

items.title: Short sellers load up against SpaceX as stock retreats back to IPO price
items.summary: About 185 million SpaceX shares are now sold short, representing roughly 29% of the company's publicly tradable float, according to S3.

items.title: Sen. Warren says Trump's CFPB overhaul has cost Americans $26.5 billion
items.summary: Sen. Elizabeth Warren says the Trump administration's rollback of CFPB rules and enforcement has cost Americans up to $26.5 billion so far.

items.title: Truth Social launches service to give Wall Street traders an edge with real-time service
items.summary: Trump Media launched an API to give paying customers real-time access to market-moving Truth Social posts.

items.title: Iran warns U.S. of Hormuz ‘red line,’ says it will retaliate if Trump carries out threats
items.summary: Trump warned that the U.S. will target Iranian infrastructure next week if negotiations do not resume.

items.title: Nvidia unveils new AI model and expands Japan’s physical AI ecosystem
items.summary: Nvidia announces new AI model, Cosmos 3 Edge, and expansion of its physical AI ecosystems in Japan.

items.title: Nvidia-backed Fireworks hits $17.5 billion valuation as companies pursue cheaper AI models
items.summary: Fireworks once relied heavily on revenue from coding startup Cursor, but has diversified in the past year as more companies reach for lower-cost AI models.

items.title: Banking Committee's Sen. Rounds liked Warsh's 'tone' in first testimony as Fed chairman
items.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh was on Capitol Hill for two days of testimony this week.

items.title: The best states for workers, job training and career opportunities in America in 2026
items.summary: These 11 U.S. states excel when it comes to the education level of their workforce, migration patterns, and workforce development programs.

items.title: IRS tax liens can be a 'kiss of death,' consumer advocate says — and they're on the rise
items.summary: Federal tax liens filed by the Internal Revenue Service can impact people's employment and their ability to access credit.

items.title: Analysis: Fed Chairman Warsh faces an inflation credibility test after Congress hearings
items.summary: The new Fed chairman avoided major stumbles in two days of testimony before the House and Senate, but faces a rapid test of his commitment to price stability.

items.title: UnitedHealth blows past estimates, hikes earnings outlook as it reins in costs
items.summary: The healthcare giant is working to stabilize margins by shrinking membership, exiting unprofitable contracts and pouring $1.5 billion into AI.

items.title: 'Not a junk rally:' How to trade the strongest small-cap stock market in three decades
items.summary: The Russell 2000 small-cap stock index is up almost 20% this year, its best first half since 1991, and investing experts say the rally can keep running.

items.title: IRS chief Bisignano: 'We should have every family enrolled in a Trump Account'
items.summary: "I think the objective is to have 70 million children under 18 to have a Trump Account," IRS CEO Frank Bigisnano said.

items.title: The best energy stocks right now as two major conflicts keep oil prices elevated
items.summary: What Brian Sullivan is hearing from energy insiders.

items.title: TSMC to invest additional $100 billion in Arizona after second-quarter profit soars 77%
items.summary: TSMC announced a second-quarter profit, following its June revenue figures released earlier this week.

items.title: 3 of our stocks rode the AI rally, while 3 others fell out of favor since last month
items.summary: Ahead of the CNBC Investing Club's July Monthly Meeting, here's a look at what moved our three top and bottom performers since our last meeting.

items.title: U.S. slaps 25% tariff on most Brazilian goods over 'unfair trade practices'
items.summary: A separate U.S. probe into forced-labor enforcement could see an additional 12.5% duty on Brazilian goods on top of the 25%, with the decision due next week.

items.title: Eli Lilly to buy psychedelics maker AtaiBeckley for $2.8 billion as experimental treatments gain traction
items.summary: The deal for AtaiBeckley gives Lilly access to experimental drugs based on DMT and MDMA.

items.title: United earnings top estimates but airline expects $6 billion in added fuel costs
items.summary: United reported higher revenue for premium, corporate and no-frills basic economy tickets and higher revenue for both domestic and international trips.

items.title: Ukrainians take to the streets after Zelenskyy ousts defense minister in surprise shake-up
items.summary: Ukrainian protesters took to the streets of Kyiv on Thursday, decrying Zelenskyy's surprise decision to dismiss Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

items.title: $100 million New Jersey deli fraud defendant Patten wants no prison time despite past conviction
items.summary: James Patten is the person to be sentenced for a scheme that sent the market capitalization of a company that owned just a small New Jersey deli soaring.

items.title: Ex-Fed advisor gets over three years in prison for lying about China ties
items.summary: The case marks one of the most prominent U.S. prosecutions alleging Chinese intelligence targeting of U.S. institutions, as Trump intensifies its pursuit of foreign economic espionage.

items.title: Trump's pick to head national intelligence, Jay Clayton, won't tell senators Biden won the 2020 election
items.summary: The Senate Intelligence Committee is considering Jay Clayton's nomination to be the director of national intelligence.

items.title: Foreign investors sweeten on Indian government bonds as equities see a sell-off
items.summary: The Indian government bond market is drawing interest from foreign investors as it readies for a likely inclusion in Bloomberg index.

items.title: Anthropic moves closer to mega-IPO as bankers line up investor meetings
items.summary: Anthropic is lining up investor meetings ahead of a potential IPO as soon as October as the AI startup behind looks to beat rival OpenAI to the public markets.

items.title: Trump blasts New York AI data center moratorium, says state should change policy 'immediately'
items.summary: New York became the first state in the U.S. to impose a ban of its kind when the governor signed the executive order Tuesday.

items.title: Public Service Loan Forgiveness has new rules — 3 changes borrowers should know about
items.summary: Recent changes to Public Service Loan Forgiveness should prompt borrowers to confirm that their repayment plan and loan type remain eligible for the relief.

items.title: Fed Chairman Warsh says he meets 'often' with Trump administration, defends independence
items.summary: Kevin Warsh said Wednesday that he speaks with Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent frequently outside their regular weekly meetings.

items.title: Jim Cramer says he needs 'cold hard' proof that AI is paying off
items.summary: CNBC's Jim Cramer said he’s looking for companies to start showing measurable financial returns from their AI investments.
